26: 		messageCache: {

52: describe("extractSessionId", () => {

57: 		};

59: 	});

65: 		};

562: describe("handleSSEEvent – tool_result truncation", () => {
563: 	it("truncates tool_result over threshold before broadcast and cache", () => {
564: 		const deps = createMockDeps();
565: 		const largeContent = "x".repeat(TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD + 1000);
566: 		const translated: RelayMessage = {
567: 			type: "tool_result",
568: 			id: "tool-1",
569: 			content: largeContent,
570: 			is_error: false,
571: 		};
572: 		vi.mocked(deps.translator.translate).mockReturnValue(translated);
573: 
574: 		const event: OpenCodeEvent = {
575: 			type: "message.part.updated",
576: 			properties: { sessionID: "active-session" },
577: 		};
578: 		handleSSEEvent(deps, event);

2: // Tests for in-memory store of full tool result content (pre-truncation).

5: import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
6: import { ToolContentStore } from "../../src/lib/tool-content-store.js";
7: 
8: // ─── Store / Retrieve ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

10: describe("store and retrieve", () => {
11: 	it("stores and retrieves content by tool ID", () => {
12: 		const store = new ToolContentStore();
13: 		store.store("tool-1", "some content");
14: 		expect(store.get("tool-1")).toBe("some content");
15: 	});
16: 
17: 	it("stores multiple entries independently", () => {
18: 		const store = new ToolContentStore();
19: 		store.store("tool-1", "content A");
20: 		store.store("tool-2", "content B");
21: 		expect(store.get("tool-1")).toBe("content A");

25: 	it("overwrites content for the same tool ID", () => {

30: 	});
31: 
32: 	it("stores content with optional sessionId", () => {

40: 
41: describe("unknown ID", () => {
42: 	it("returns undefined for unknown tool ID", () => {
43: 		const store = new ToolContentStore();
44: 		expect(store.get("nonexistent")).toBeUndefined();
45: 	});
46: 
47: 	it("returns undefined after clearing a session that contained the ID", () => {
48: 		const store = new ToolContentStore();
49: 		store.store("tool-1", "content", "session-1");

53: });

56: 
57: describe("clearSession", () => {

59: 		const store = new ToolContentStore();

62: 		store.store("tool-3", "c", "session-2");

64: 		store.clearSession("session-1");

71: 	it("does not throw when clearing a nonexistent session", () => {

74: 	});

76: 	it("updates size after clearing session", () => {

82: 		store.clearSession("session-1");

100: describe("eviction when over capacity", () => {
101: 	it("evicts oldest entry when exceeding maxEntries", () => {
102: 		const store = new ToolContentStore(3);
103: 
104: 		store.store("tool-1", "first");

108: 		expect(store.size).toBe(3);
109: 		expect(store.get("tool-1")).toBe("first");

111: 		// Adding a 4th should evict the oldest (tool-1)

133: 		const store = new ToolContentStore();

163: 		const store = new ToolContentStore();

<content>1: // ─── truncateContent / truncateToolResult Unit Tests ─────────────────────────
2: // Tests for the content truncation utility used with large tool results.
3: // Verifies: threshold behavior, edge cases, tool_result message truncation.
4: 
5: import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
6: import {
7: 	TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD,
8: 	truncateContent,
9: 	truncateToolResult,
10: } from "../../src/lib/truncate-content.js";
11: import type { RelayMessage } from "../../src/lib/types.js";
12: 
13: // ─── truncateContent ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: 
15: describe("truncateContent", () => {
16: 	it("returns content unchanged when under threshold", () => {
17: 		const result = truncateContent("short content");
18: 		expect(result.content).toBe("short content");
19: 		expect(result.isTruncated).toBe(false);
20: 		expect(result.fullContentLength).toBeUndefined();
21: 	});
22: 
23: 	it("truncates content over threshold", () => {
24: 		const longContent = "x".repeat(TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD + 100);
25: 		const result = truncateContent(longContent);
26: 
27: 		expect(result.isTruncated).toBe(true);
28: 		expect(result.fullContentLength).toBe(TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD + 100);
29: 		expect(result.content.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD);
30: 		expect(result.content).toContain("[truncated]");
31: 	});
32: 
33: 	it("returns content unchanged at exact threshold", () => {
34: 		const exactContent = "y".repeat(TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD);
35: 		const result = truncateContent(exactContent);
36: 
37: 		expect(result.content).toBe(exactContent);
38: 		expect(result.isTruncated).toBe(false);
39: 		expect(result.fullContentLength).toBeUndefined();
40: 	});
41: 
42: 	it("handles empty string", () => {
43: 		const result = truncateContent("");
44: 		expect(result.content).toBe("");
45: 		expect(result.isTruncated).toBe(false);
46: 		expect(result.fullContentLength).toBeUndefined();
47: 	});
48: 
49: 	it("accepts custom threshold", () => {
50: 		const content = "abcdefghij"; // 10 chars
51: 		const result = truncateContent(content, 5);
52: 
53: 		expect(result.isTruncated).toBe(true);
54: 		expect(result.fullContentLength).toBe(10);
55: 		expect(result.content.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(5);
56: 	});
57: 
58: 	it("preserves content exactly at custom threshold", () => {
59: 		const content = "abcde"; // 5 chars
60: 		const result = truncateContent(content, 5);
61: 
62: 		expect(result.content).toBe("abcde");
63: 		expect(result.isTruncated).toBe(false);
64: 	});
65: 
66: 	it("exports TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD as 50_000", () => {
67: 		expect(TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD).toBe(50_000);
68: 	});
69: });
70: 
71: // ─── truncateToolResult ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: 
73: describe("truncateToolResult", () => {
74: 	it("truncates large tool_result content and returns full content", () => {
75: 		const largeContent = "z".repeat(TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD + 500);
76: 		const msg: RelayMessage = {
77: 			type: "tool_result",
78: 			id: "tool-42",
79: 			content: largeContent,
80: 			is_error: false,
81: 		};
82: 
83: 		const result = truncateToolResult(msg);
84: 
85: 		// Truncated message should have truncated content
86: 		expect(result.truncated.type).toBe("tool_result");
87: 		expect(result.truncated.content.length).toBeLessThan(largeContent.length);
88: 		expect(result.truncated.isTruncated).toBe(true);
89: 		expect(result.truncated.fullContentLength).toBe(largeContent.length);
90: 
91: 		// Full content should be returned
92: 		expect(result.fullContent).toBe(largeContent);
93: 	});
94: 
95: 	it("passes through small tool_result unchanged", () => {
96: 		const smallContent = "small output";
97: 		const msg: RelayMessage = {
98: 			type: "tool_result",
99: 			id: "tool-99",
100: 			content: smallContent,
101: 			is_error: false,
102: 		};
103: 
104: 		const result = truncateToolResult(msg);
105: 
106: 		expect(result.truncated.content).toBe(smallContent);
107: 		expect(result.truncated.isTruncated).toBeUndefined();
108: 		expect(result.truncated.fullContentLength).toBeUndefined();
109: 		expect(result.fullContent).toBeUndefined();
110: 	});
111: 
112: 	it("preserves all other fields on the message", () => {
113: 		const msg: RelayMessage = {
114: 			type: "tool_result",
115: 			id: "tool-7",
116: 			content: "x".repeat(TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD + 1),
117: 			is_error: true,
118: 			messageId: "msg-123",
119: 		};
120: 
121: 		const result = truncateToolResult(msg);
122: 
123: 		expect(result.truncated.id).toBe("tool-7");
124: 		expect(result.truncated.is_error).toBe(true);
125: 		expect(result.truncated.messageId).toBe("msg-123");
126: 	});
127: });

2: // Tests for the sliding-window rate limiter utility.
3: // Verifies: allow/reject behavior, retryAfterMs, independent client tracking,
4: // remaining count, cleanup of stale entries, edge cases.
5: 
6: import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest";
7: import { RateLimiter } from "../../src/lib/rate-limiter.js";
8: 
9: describe("RateLimiter", () => {
10: 	// ─── Allows messages under the limit ────────────────────────────────────
11: 
12: 	it("allows messages under the limit", () => {
13: 		let clock = 0;
14: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({ now: () => clock });
15: 
16: 		for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
17: 			const result = limiter.check("client-a");
18: 			expect(result.allowed).toBe(true);
19: 			expect(result.retryAfterMs).toBeUndefined();
20: 			clock += 100; // small time increments within window
21: 		}
22: 	});
23: 
24: 	// ─── Rejects the 6th message within the window ──────────────────────────
25: 
26: 	it("rejects the 6th message with correct retryAfterMs", () => {
27: 		let clock = 0;
28: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({ now: () => clock });
29: 
30: 		// Send 5 messages at known times
31: 		for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
32: 			limiter.check("client-a");
33: 			clock += 100; // messages at t=0, 100, 200, 300, 400
34: 		}
35: 
36: 		// clock is now 500; 6th message should be rejected
37: 		const result = limiter.check("client-a");
38: 		expect(result.allowed).toBe(false);
39: 		// Oldest message is at t=0, window is 10_000ms.
40: 		// retryAfterMs = (0 + 10_000) - 500 = 9500
41: 		expect(result.retryAfterMs).toBe(9500);
42: 	});
43: 
44: 	// ─── After the window expires, allows messages again ────────────────────
45: 
46: 	it("allows messages again after the window expires", () => {
47: 		let clock = 0;
48: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({ now: () => clock });
49: 
50: 		// Fill up the limit
51: 		for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
52: 			limiter.check("client-a");
53: 			clock += 100;
54: 		}
55: 
56: 		// Rejected at t=500
57: 		expect(limiter.check("client-a").allowed).toBe(false);
58: 
59: 		// Advance past the window for the oldest message (t=0 + 10_000 = 10_000)
60: 		clock = 10_001;
61: 		const result = limiter.check("client-a");
62: 		expect(result.allowed).toBe(true);
63: 	});
64: 
65: 	// ─── Independent client tracking ────────────────────────────────────────
66: 
67: 	it("tracks clients independently", () => {
68: 		let clock = 0;
69: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({ now: () => clock });
70: 
71: 		// Fill up client A
72: 		for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
73: 			limiter.check("client-a");
74: 			clock += 10;
75: 		}
76: 
77: 		// Client A is at limit
78: 		expect(limiter.check("client-a").allowed).toBe(false);
79: 
80: 		// Client B should still be allowed
81: 		const result = limiter.check("client-b");
82: 		expect(result.allowed).toBe(true);
83: 	});
84: 
85: 	// ─── remaining() ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
86: 
87: 	it("remaining() returns correct count", () => {
88: 		let clock = 0;
89: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({ now: () => clock });
90: 
91: 		expect(limiter.remaining("client-a")).toBe(5);
92: 
93: 		limiter.check("client-a");
94: 		clock += 10;
95: 		expect(limiter.remaining("client-a")).toBe(4);
96: 
97: 		limiter.check("client-a");
98: 		clock += 10;
99: 		expect(limiter.remaining("client-a")).toBe(3);
100: 
101: 		limiter.check("client-a");
102: 		clock += 10;
103: 		limiter.check("client-a");
104: 		clock += 10;
105: 		limiter.check("client-a");
106: 		clock += 10;
107: 		expect(limiter.remaining("client-a")).toBe(0);
108: 	});
109: 
110: 	// ─── remaining() for new/empty client ───────────────────────────────────
111: 
112: 	it("remaining() returns full count for unknown client", () => {
113: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({ now: () => 0 });
114: 		expect(limiter.remaining("never-seen")).toBe(5);
115: 	});
116: 
117: 	// ─── cleanup() ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
118: 
119: 	it("cleanup() removes stale clients", () => {
120: 		let clock = 0;
121: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({ now: () => clock });
122: 
123: 		// Client A sends a message at t=0
124: 		limiter.check("client-a");
125: 		// Client B sends a message at t=0
126: 		limiter.check("client-b");
127: 
128: 		// Advance past the window
129: 		clock = 11_000;
130: 
131: 		// Client B sends another message — keeps it alive
132: 		limiter.check("client-b");
133: 
134: 		limiter.cleanup();
135: 
136: 		// Client A should have been cleaned up — remaining returns full count
137: 		expect(limiter.remaining("client-a")).toBe(5);
138: 
139: 		// Client B still tracked — just sent at t=11_000
140: 		expect(limiter.remaining("client-b")).toBe(4);
141: 	});
142: 
143: 	// ─── Custom config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
144: 
145: 	it("respects custom maxMessages and windowMs", () => {
146: 		let clock = 0;
147: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({
148: 			maxMessages: 2,
149: 			windowMs: 1000,
150: 			now: () => clock,
151: 		});
152: 
153: 		expect(limiter.check("c").allowed).toBe(true);
154: 		clock += 10;
155: 		expect(limiter.check("c").allowed).toBe(true);
156: 		clock += 10;
157: 
158: 		const rejected = limiter.check("c");
159: 		expect(rejected.allowed).toBe(false);
160: 		// Oldest at t=0, window=1000 → retryAfterMs = (0 + 1000) - 20 = 980
161: 		expect(rejected.retryAfterMs).toBe(980);
162: 	});
163: 
164: 	// ─── retryAfterMs decreases as time passes ──────────────────────────────
165: 
166: 	it("retryAfterMs decreases as time passes", () => {
167: 		let clock = 0;
168: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({ now: () => clock });
169: 
170: 		for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
171: 			limiter.check("c");
172: 		}
173: 
174: 		// All 5 at t=0; rejected at t=0
175: 		const r1 = limiter.check("c");
176: 		expect(r1.allowed).toBe(false);
177: 		expect(r1.retryAfterMs).toBe(10_000);
178: 
179: 		// Advance 3 seconds
180: 		clock = 3000;
181: 		const r2 = limiter.check("c");
182: 		expect(r2.allowed).toBe(false);
183: 		expect(r2.retryAfterMs).toBe(7000);
184: 	});
185: 
186: 	// ─── Sliding window slides correctly ────────────────────────────────────
187: 
188: 	it("slides the window — only oldest message expires first", () => {
189: 		let clock = 0;
190: 		const limiter = new RateLimiter({
191: 			maxMessages: 3,
192: 			windowMs: 1000,
193: 			now: () => clock,
194: 		});
195: 
196: 		// Messages at t=0, 200, 400
197: 		limiter.check("c");
198: 		clock = 200;
199: 		limiter.check("c");
200: 		clock = 400;
201: 		limiter.check("c");
202: 
203: 		// At t=400 — full
204: 		expect(limiter.check("c").allowed).toBe(false);
205: 
206: 		// At t=1001 — oldest (t=0) expires, so one slot opens
207: 		clock = 1001;
208: 		expect(limiter.check("c").allowed).toBe(true);
209: 
210: 		// Now window has: t=200, 400, 1001 — full again
211: 		expect(limiter.check("c").allowed).toBe(false);
212: 	});
213: });

<content>1: // ─── Unit Tests: StorageMonitor (Ticket 6.2 AC8) ────────────────────────────
2: //
3: // Tests:
4: // T1:  Emits `low_disk_space` when available space drops below threshold
5: // T2:  Does NOT emit when space is above threshold
6: // T3:  Emits `disk_space_ok` when space recovers
7: // T4:  Does NOT emit `disk_space_ok` on first check if space is above threshold
8: // T5:  Does NOT re-emit `low_disk_space` on consecutive low checks (transition only)
9: // T6:  `stop()` is idempotent (calling stop twice doesn't throw)
10: // T7:  Custom threshold works
11: 
12: import { describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
13: import type {
14: 	DiskSpaceOkEvent,
15: 	LowDiskSpaceEvent,
16: } from "../../src/lib/storage-monitor.js";
17: import { StorageMonitor } from "../../src/lib/storage-monitor.js";
18: 
19: // ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20: 
21: /** Create an injectable _statfs that returns the given available bytes */
22: function mockStatfs(availableBytes: number) {
23: 	return vi.fn(async (_path: string) => ({ available: availableBytes }));
24: }
25: 
26: /** Create a _statfs that resolves on demand via returned triggers */
27: function mockStatfsControlled(sequence: number[]) {
28: 	let callIndex = 0;
29: 	const calls: Array<(v: { available: number }) => void> = [];
30: 	const fn = vi.fn(
31: 		(_path: string) =>
32: 			new Promise<{ available: number }>((resolve) => {
33: 				calls.push(resolve);
34: 			}),
35: 	);
36: 
37: 	/** Resolve the next pending call with the next value in the sequence */
38: 	function next() {
39: 		const resolve = calls.shift();
40: 		if (!resolve) throw new Error("No pending statfs call to resolve");
41: 		const available = sequence[callIndex] ?? sequence[sequence.length - 1]!;
42: 		callIndex++;
43: 		resolve({ available });
44: 	}
45: 
46: 	return { fn, next };
47: }
48: 
49: // ─── Tests ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50: 
51: describe("Ticket 6.2 AC8 — StorageMonitor", () => {
52: 	// ─── T1: Emits low_disk_space when below threshold ──────────────────
53: 
54: 	describe("T1: Emits low_disk_space when available space drops below threshold", () => {
55: 		it("emits low_disk_space on first check when below threshold", async () => {
56: 			const threshold = 100 * 1024 * 1024; // 100MB
57: 			const belowThreshold = threshold - 1;
58: 			const statfs = mockStatfs(belowThreshold);
59: 
60: 			const monitor = new StorageMonitor({
61: 				path: "/tmp",
62: 				thresholdBytes: threshold,
63: 				intervalMs: 60_000,
64: 				_statfs: statfs,
65: 			});
66: 
67: 			const events: LowDiskSpaceEvent[] = [];
68: 			monitor.on("low_disk_space", (data: LowDiskSpaceEvent) =>
69: 				events.push(data),
70: 			);
71: 
72: 			monitor.start();
73: 			await vi.waitFor(() => expect(statfs).toHaveBeenCalled());
74: 			await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 10));
75: 			monitor.stop();
76: 
77: 			expect(events).toHaveLength(1);
78: 			expect(events[0]).toEqual({
79: 				availableBytes: belowThreshold,
80: 				thresholdBytes: threshold,
81: 			});
82: 		});
83: 
84: 		it("emits low_disk_space when space is exactly 0", async () => {
85: 			const statfs = mockStatfs(0);
86: 
87: 			const monitor = new StorageMonitor({
88: 				path: "/tmp",
89: 				_statfs: statfs,
90: 			});
91: 
92: 			const events: LowDiskSpaceEvent[] = [];
93: 			monitor.on("low_disk_space", (data: LowDiskSpaceEvent) =>
94: 				events.push(data),
95: 			);
96: 
97: 			monitor.start();
98: 			await vi.waitFor(() => expect(statfs).toHaveBeenCalled());
99: 			await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 10));
100: 			monitor.stop();
101: 
102: 			expect(events).toHaveLength(1);
103: 			expect(events[0]!.availableBytes).toBe(0);
104: 		});
105: 	});
106: 
107: 	// ─── T2: Does NOT emit when space is above threshold ────────────────
108: 
109: 	describe("T2: Does NOT emit when space is above threshold", () => {
110: 		it("no events emitted when space is plentiful", async () => {
111: 			const threshold = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
112: 			const aboveThreshold = threshold + 1;
113: 			const statfs = mockStatfs(aboveThreshold);
114: 
115: 			const monitor = new StorageMonitor({
116: 				path: "/tmp",
117: 				thresholdBytes: threshold,
118: 				intervalMs: 60_000,
119: 				_statfs: statfs,
120: 			});
121: 
122: 			const lowEvents: LowDiskSpaceEvent[] = [];
123: 			const okEvents: DiskSpaceOkEvent[] = [];
124: 			monitor.on("low_disk_space", (data: LowDiskSpaceEvent) =>
125: 				lowEvents.push(data),
126: 			);
127: 			monitor.on("disk_space_ok", (data: DiskSpaceOkEvent) =>
128: 				okEvents.push(data),
129: 			);
130: 
131: 			monitor.start();
132: 			await vi.waitFor(() => expect(statfs).toHaveBeenCalled());
133: 			await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 10));
134: 			monitor.stop();
135: 
136: 			expect(lowEvents).toHaveLength(0);
137: 			expect(okEvents).toHaveLength(0);
138: 		});
139: 
140: 		it("no events when exactly at threshold", async () => {
141: 			const threshold = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
142: 			const statfs = mockStatfs(threshold);
143: 
144: 			const monitor = new StorageMonitor({
145: 				path: "/tmp",
146: 				thresholdBytes: threshold,
147: 				intervalMs: 60_000,
148: 				_statfs: statfs,
149: 			});
150: 
151: 			const lowEvents: LowDiskSpaceEvent[] = [];
152: 			monitor.on("low_disk_space", (data: LowDiskSpaceEvent) =>
153: 				lowEvents.push(data),
154: 			);
155: 
156: 			monitor.start();
157: 			await vi.waitFor(() => expect(statfs).toHaveBeenCalled());
158: 			await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 10));
159: 			monitor.stop();

161: 			// At threshold exactly means NOT below — should not emit
162: 			expect(lowEvents).toHaveLength(0);
163: 		});
164: 	});

166: 	// ─── T3: Emits disk_space_ok when space recovers ────────────────────
167: 
168: 	describe("T3: Emits disk_space_ok when space recovers", () => {
169: 		it("emits disk_space_ok after low_disk_space then recovery", async () => {
170: 			const threshold = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
171: 			const low = threshold - 1;
172: 			const high = threshold + 1_000_000;
173: 			const { fn: statfs, next } = mockStatfsControlled([low, high]);
174: 
175: 			const monitor = new StorageMonitor({
176: 				path: "/tmp",
177: 				thresholdBytes: threshold,
178: 				intervalMs: 10,
179: 				_statfs: statfs,
180: 			});
181: 
182: 			const lowEvents: LowDiskSpaceEvent[] = [];
183: 			const okEvents: DiskSpaceOkEvent[] = [];
184: 			monitor.on("low_disk_space", (data: LowDiskSpaceEvent) =>
185: 				lowEvents.push(data),
186: 			);
187: 			monitor.on("disk_space_ok", (data: DiskSpaceOkEvent) =>
188: 				okEvents.push(data),
189: 			);
190: 
191: 			monitor.start();
192: 
193: 			// First check: low
194: 			await vi.waitFor(() => expect(statfs).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1));
195: 			next();
196: 			await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 5));
197: 
198: 			// Second check: high (recovery)
199: 			await vi.waitFor(() => expect(statfs).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(2));
200: 			next();
201: 			await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 5));

203: 			monitor.stop();

205: 			expect(lowEvents).toHaveLength(1);

208: 		});
209: 	});

211: 	// ─── T4: Does NOT emit disk_space_ok on first check if above threshold ─
212: 
213: 	describe("T4: Does NOT emit disk_space_ok on first check if space is above threshold", () => {
214: 		it("no disk_space_ok when first check is above threshold", async () => {
215: 			const threshold = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
216: 			const statfs = mockStatfs(threshold + 1_000_000);
217: 
218: 			const monitor = new StorageMonitor({
219: 				path: "/tmp",
220: 				thresholdBytes: threshold,
221: 				intervalMs: 60_000,

223: 			});
224: 
225: 			const okEvents: DiskSpaceOkEvent[] = [];

227: 				okEvents.push(data),

230: 			monitor.start();

255: 
256: 			const lowEvents: LowDiskSpaceEvent[] = [];

492: 
493: 		// s1 is older. Now record another event to s1 — should update its access time
494: 		await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 10));
495: 		cache.recordEvent("s1", { type: "delta", text: "more for s1" });
496: 
497: 		// s2 should now be oldest

<content>1: // ─── Client-side Rate Limit Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Tests for the client-side message queue in ws.svelte.ts.
3: // Verifies: immediate sends under limit, queuing at limit, drain timer,
4: // queue replacement, and non-message bypass.
5: 
6: import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest";
7: 
8: // ─── Hoisted mocks (run before imports) ─────────────────────────────────────
9: const { showToastMock, sentMessages } = vi.hoisted(() => {
10: 	const showToastMock = vi.fn();
11: 	const sentMessages: string[] = [];
12: 
13: 	class MockWebSocket {
14: 		static readonly OPEN = 1;
15: 		static readonly CLOSED = 3;
16: 		readyState = MockWebSocket.OPEN;
17: 		private listeners: Record<string, Array<(ev?: unknown) => void>> = {};
18: 
19: 		send(data: string): void {
20: 			sentMessages.push(data);
21: 		}
22: 
23: 		addEventListener(event: string, fn: (ev?: unknown) => void): void {
24: 			if (!this.listeners[event]) this.listeners[event] = [];
25: 			this.listeners[event]!.push(fn);
26: 		}
27: 
28: 		close(): void {
29: 			this.readyState = MockWebSocket.CLOSED;
30: 		}
31: 
32: 		_fire(event: string, data?: unknown): void {
33: 			for (const fn of this.listeners[event] ?? []) {
34: 				fn(data);
35: 			}
36: 		}
37: 	}
38: 
39: 	// Install mock WebSocket globally before any module imports
40: 	Object.defineProperty(globalThis, "WebSocket", {
41: 		value: MockWebSocket,
42: 		writable: true,
43: 		configurable: true,
44: 	});
45: 
46: 	// Mock window.location so connect() can build the URL
47: 	if (typeof globalThis.window === "undefined") {
48: 		Object.defineProperty(globalThis, "window", {
49: 			value: {
50: 				location: { protocol: "http:", host: "localhost:3000" },
51: 			},
52: 			writable: true,
53: 			configurable: true,
54: 		});
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	return { showToastMock, sentMessages, MockWebSocket };
58: });
59: 
60: // ─── Mock ui.svelte.js to capture showToast calls ───────────────────────────
61: vi.mock("../../src/lib/public/stores/ui.svelte.js", () => ({
62: 	showToast: showToastMock,
63: 	showBanner: vi.fn(),
64: 	removeBanner: vi.fn(),
65: 	setClientCount: vi.fn(),
66: }));
67: 
68: import {
69: 	_resetRateLimit,
70: 	connect,
71: 	disconnect,
72: 	wsSend,
73: } from "../../src/lib/public/stores/ws.svelte.js";
74: 
75: // ─── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76: 
77: /** Parsed version of the last sent message. */
78: function lastSent(): Record<string, unknown> | undefined {
79: 	const raw = sentMessages[sentMessages.length - 1];
80: 	return raw ? (JSON.parse(raw) as Record<string, unknown>) : undefined;
81: }
82: 
83: let clock: number;
84: 
85: beforeEach(() => {
86: 	vi.useFakeTimers();
87: 	clock = 0;
88: 	sentMessages.length = 0;
89: 	showToastMock.mockClear();
90: 	_resetRateLimit({ now: () => clock });
91: 
92: 	// Establish a "connected" WebSocket so rawSend works.
93: 	// connect() creates a new MockWebSocket; we fire "open" to mark it connected.
94: 	connect();
95: 	// The connect function adds event listeners; find the mock and fire "open"
96: 	// We need to get the MockWebSocket instance. Since connect() assigns to _ws,
97: 	// and our MockWebSocket is used, we access it via the global constructor calls.
98: 	// Actually, connect() creates `new WebSocket(url)` which is our MockWebSocket.
99: 	// The "open" listener sets status. Let's fire it by accessing the instance.
100: });
101: 
102: afterEach(() => {
103: 	disconnect();
104: 	vi.useRealTimers();
105: });
106: 
107: // ─── Helper to establish the WS connection ──────────────────────────────────
108: // connect() creates a MockWebSocket but we need to fire "open" on it.
109: // Since we can't directly access _ws, we verify sends work by checking sentMessages.
110: 
111: // Actually, connect() constructs a WebSocket internally. Our MockWebSocket captures
112: // addEventListener calls. We need to trigger the open event. Since the mock is
113: // simple, let's just verify the flow by testing wsSend directly — if _ws is set
114: // and readyState is OPEN (default in our mock), rawSend works.
115: 
116: // ─── Tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
117: 
118: describe("wsSend client-side rate limiting", () => {
119: 	// ─── Non-message types bypass rate limiting ─────────────────────────────
120: 
121: 	describe("non-message types", () => {
122: 		it("sends control messages immediately without rate limiting", () => {
123: 			for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
124: 				wsSend({ type: "subscribe", channel: "test" });
125: 			}
126: 			// All 10 should have been sent (no queuing)
127: 			expect(sentMessages).toHaveLength(10);
128: 			expect(showToastMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
129: 		});
130: 
131: 		it("sends permission responses immediately", () => {
132: 			wsSend({ type: "permission_response", allow: true });
133: 			expect(sentMessages).toHaveLength(1);
134: 		});
135: 	});
136: 
137: 	// ─── Under limit — immediate send ───────────────────────────────────────
138: 
139: 	describe("under rate limit", () => {
140: 		it("sends up to MAX_MESSAGES immediately", () => {
141: 			for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
142: 				wsSend({ type: "message", text: `msg ${i}` });
143: 				clock += 100;
144: 			}
145: 			expect(sentMessages).toHaveLength(5);
146: 			expect(showToastMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
147: 		});
148: 
149: 		it("records timestamps for sent messages", () => {
150: 			wsSend({ type: "message", text: "a" });
151: 			clock += 100;
152: 			wsSend({ type: "message", text: "b" });
153: 			expect(sentMessages).toHaveLength(2);
154: 		});
155: 	});
156: 
157: 	// ─── At limit — queuing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
158: 
159: 	describe("at rate limit", () => {
160: 		function fillLimit(): void {
161: 			for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
162: 				wsSend({ type: "message", text: `msg ${i}` });
163: 				clock += 100;
164: 			}
165: 		}
166: 
167: 		it("queues the 6th message and shows a toast", () => {
168: 			fillLimit();
169: 			sentMessages.length = 0; // clear to see only queued sends
170: 
171: 			wsSend({ type: "message", text: "queued" });
172: 			// Should NOT have been sent yet
173: 			expect(sentMessages).toHaveLength(0);
174: 			expect(showToastMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
175: 				"Message queued — sending shortly",
176: 				{ variant: "warn" },
177: 			);
178: 		});
179: 
180: 		it("drains the queued message after the window slides", () => {
181: 			fillLimit();
182: 			// Messages sent at t=0, 100, 200, 300, 400; clock is now 500
183: 			sentMessages.length = 0;
184: 
185: 			wsSend({ type: "message", text: "queued" });
186: 			expect(sentMessages).toHaveLength(0);
187: 
188: 			// Oldest timestamp is 0. Window expires at 0 + 10_000 = 10_000.
189: 			// Timer delay = max(0, 10_000 - 500) = 9500.
190: 			// Advance clock to when the timer fires.
191: 			clock = 10_001;
192: 			vi.advanceTimersByTime(9500);
193:
